questions.q: In which year was the K. Viswanath film Sankarabharanam released?
questions.explanation: K. Viswanath's landmark film Sankarabharanam was released in 1980.

questions.q: K. Viswanath was primarily associated with which language film industry?
questions.explanation: K. Viswanath was a celebrated director of Telugu cinema.

questions.q: Which of these films is K. Viswanath best known for directing?
questions.explanation: Sankarabharanam (1980) is K. Viswanath's most celebrated directorial work.

questions.q: K. Viswanath's films are most strongly rooted in which themes?
questions.explanation: K. Viswanath was renowned for films deeply rooted in classical Indian music and dance.

questions.q: In which year was K. Viswanath born?
questions.explanation: K. Viswanath was born in 1930.

questions.q: India's highest award in cinema, the Dadasaheb Phalke Award, was conferred on K. Viswanath in which year?
questions.explanation: K. Viswanath received the Dadasaheb Phalke Award in 2016.

questions.q: Which 1983 K. Viswanath film centred on a gifted but tragic dancer?
questions.explanation: Sagara Sangamam (1983) was K. Viswanath's acclaimed film about a brilliant but doomed dancer.

questions.q: Which 1986 K. Viswanath film told the story of a simple-minded man with a pure heart?
questions.explanation: Swathi Muthyam (1986) was K. Viswanath's film about an innocent, pure-hearted man.

questions.q: Apart from directing, in which role did K. Viswanath also become well known in his later career?
questions.explanation: In his later career K. Viswanath also appeared as a character actor in many Telugu and Tamil films.

questions.q: In which year did the veteran director K. Viswanath pass away?
questions.explanation: K. Viswanath passed away in 2023.

questions.q: Which classical Carnatic raga gives the K. Viswanath film Sankarabharanam its title?
questions.explanation: The film is named after the Carnatic raga Shankarabharanam, central to its musical theme.

questions.q: Who composed the music for K. Viswanath's Sankarabharanam (1980)?
questions.explanation: The classical score of Sankarabharanam was composed by K. V. Mahadevan.

questions.q: Which composer scored K. Viswanath's films Sagara Sangamam and Swathi Muthyam?
questions.explanation: Ilaiyaraaja composed the celebrated scores for both Sagara Sangamam and Swathi Muthyam.

questions.q: Which leading actor played the central dancer in K. Viswanath's Sagara Sangamam (1983)?
questions.explanation: Kamal Haasan played the gifted dancer Balakrishna in Sagara Sangamam.

questions.q: Which actor portrayed the simple-hearted protagonist in K. Viswanath's Swathi Muthyam (1986)?
questions.explanation: Kamal Haasan played the innocent protagonist Sivaiah in Swathi Muthyam.

questions.q: K. Viswanath's Swathi Muthyam (1986) was India's official entry to the Academy Awards in which category?
questions.explanation: Swathi Muthyam was India's official entry for the Best Foreign Language Film at the Oscars.

questions.q: Which 1988 K. Viswanath film, centred on classical dance, starred Venkatesh and Bhanupriya?
questions.explanation: Swarnakamalam (1988) was K. Viswanath's dance-themed film starring Venkatesh and Bhanupriya.

questions.q: Which 1985 Hindi film did K. Viswanath direct as a reworking of his classical-music hit Sankarabharanam?
questions.explanation: K. Viswanath directed the 1985 Hindi film Sur Sangam, a reworking of his Telugu classic Sankarabharanam, starring Girish Karnad and Jaya Prada.

questions.q: Which actor played the egotistical classical singer Anantharama Sharma in K. Viswanath's Swati Kiranam (1992)?
questions.explanation: Mammootty played the proud music maestro Anantharama Sharma in K. Viswanath's Swati Kiranam (1992).

questions.q: Besides directing, K. Viswanath had what notable creative role on many of his own films?
questions.explanation: K. Viswanath also wrote the screenplays for many of his films, shaping their story and dialogue.

questions.q: K. Viswanath began his career in the film industry in which technical department?
questions.explanation: K. Viswanath started out as a sound recordist before becoming a director.

questions.q: Sankarabharanam, directed by K. Viswanath, told the story of a master practitioner of which art form?
questions.explanation: Sankarabharanam followed an aging Carnatic classical singer and his bond with a young woman.

questions.q: Which actor played the central classical singer Sankara Sastry in K. Viswanath's Sankarabharanam?
questions.explanation: J. V. Somayajulu portrayed the classical singer Sankara Sastry in Sankarabharanam.

questions.q: Which actor played the lead, opposite Meenakshi Seshadri, in K. Viswanath's 1992 film Aapadbandhavudu?
questions.explanation: Chiranjeevi starred opposite Meenakshi Seshadri in K. Viswanath's Aapadbandhavudu (1992).

questions.q: K. Viswanath was popularly given which honorific title celebrating his art-rooted cinema?
questions.explanation: K. Viswanath was widely revered with the honorific title 'Kalatapasvi'.

questions.q: In which Mani Ratnam film did K. Viswanath play a prominent acting role in 1995?
questions.explanation: K. Viswanath appeared as an actor in Mani Ratnam's 1995 film Bombay.

questions.q: K. Viswanath's Sankarabharanam (1980) won the National Film Award in which category?
questions.explanation: Sankarabharanam won the National Film Award for Best Music Direction (K. V. Mahadevan), among other honours.

questions.q: Which 1986 K. Viswanath film celebrating music and poetry gave its name to the lyricist Sirivennela Seetharama Sastry?
questions.explanation: Sirivennela (1986) was K. Viswanath's film celebrating music and poetry, and lyricist Seetharama Sastry adopted its name.

questions.q: Which 1981 K. Viswanath film attacked untouchability through the love between a Brahmin dancer and a Harijan flautist?
questions.explanation: Saptapadi (1981) by K. Viswanath denounced untouchability through the romance of an upper-caste dancer, Hema, and Murali, a Harijan who plays the flute in her troupe; it won the National Film Award for Best Feature Film on National Integration.

questions.q: K. Viswanath's 1976 film Siri Siri Muvva was later remade by him in Hindi in 1979 under which title?
questions.explanation: K. Viswanath remade Siri Siri Muvva as the Hindi film Sargam (1979), starring Jaya Prada and Rishi Kapoor.
